title: West Okement Valley 63926 Memorial
narrative: The PB4Y-1 Liberator was the US Navy's version of the B-24, deployed to RAF Dunkeswell in Devon for anti-submarine operations over the Bay of Biscay. Squadron VB-110 operated these long-range maritime patrol aircraft against German U-boats threatening Allied Atlantic convoys. On 28 December 1943, Liberator 63926 was in transit when it encountered poor visibility over Dartmoor. The aircraft flew into the steep-sided West Okement Valley, crashing between the Slipper Stones and Steng-a-Tor on the western side, opposite the south end of Black-a-Tor Copse. All ten crew members were killed instantly. The crash site is remote and remains littered with wreckage fragments. Though this is a US Navy rather than RAF Bomber Command aircraft, it represents the broader Allied bomber operations that used Devon bases extensively.

crew.name: William W. Parish
crew.fate_detail: Killed when the Liberator flew into the steep-sided West Okement Valley in poor visibility. Had radioed control at 22:15 with an ETA of 22:20, minutes before the crash.
crew.bio: Lieutenant and pilot of the PB4Y-1 Liberator from VB-110, US Navy. Was returning from an anti-submarine patrol mission over the Bay of Biscay.

crew.name: Donald M. Lyons
crew.fate_detail: Killed in the crash in West Okement Valley.

crew.name: Arthur J. Stork
crew.bio: Aviation Machinist's Mate 2nd Class serving as flight engineer.

crew.name: John E. Shaffer
crew.bio: Aviation Machinist's Mate 2nd Class serving as flight engineer.

crew.name: Leo M. Davenport
crew.bio: Radioman 2nd Class serving as radio operator.

crew.name: John F. Benson
crew.bio: Radioman 3rd Class serving as radio operator.

crew.name: Alfred J. Roddy Jr.
crew.bio: Aviation Machinist's Mate 3rd Class serving as gunner.

crew.name: Charles A. Reynard
crew.bio: Aviation Machinist's Mate 3rd Class serving as ordnance operator.

crew.name: Dwight E. Nash
crew.bio: Aviation Machinist's Mate 3rd Class serving as gunner.

cause_of_loss: Aircraft flew into high ground in poor visibility during transit
memorial_details: For nearly 80 years, the crash site had no formal memorial. In May 2022, a memorial plaque was installed on the ridge above the valley. The stone was brought to the location by a Royal Navy Commando helicopter, given the remoteness of the site. The memorial is accessible via a walk from Meldon Reservoir car park, approximately 3 miles. Wreckage remains visible at the crash site below.

external_narratives.summary: Detailed account of the PB4Y-1 Liberator 63926 crash in West Okement Valley. The aircraft departed Dunkeswell on 28 December 1943 with 14 other aircraft from Air Wing 7 to engage enemy destroyers in the Bay of Biscay. At 22:15 the pilot radioed an ETA of 22:20, but minutes later the aircraft struck the hillside in low cloud.
key_details: Aircraft was part of a 15-aircraft mission from Air Wing 7
Pilot Lt Parish radioed control at 22:15 with ETA of 22:20
Aircraft still carried a full bomb load for unknown reasons
Wreckage was partly removed in 1960s; Devon Aircraft Research and Recovery Team examined site in 1973
Considerable wreckage still visible at site over 80 years later

external_narratives.summary: Legendary Dartmoor's account of the 'Lost Liberator' crash on Dartmoor. Describes the remote crash site in West Okement Valley and the long campaign to get a memorial erected for the ten US Navy crew members who died.
key_details: Describes the remote and atmospheric crash site location
Wreckage strewn from near ridge top down to the river

external_narratives.summary: Official Royal Navy news release about the May 2022 memorial installation. Commando helicopters from the Royal Navy delivered the memorial stones to the remote Dartmoor crash site, enabling a formal dedication ahead of the 80th anniversary of the crash.
key_details: Royal Navy Commando helicopter transported the memorial stone
Official military involvement in memorial installation

external_narratives.summary: Walking guide to the PB4Y-1 Liberator 63926 crash site on Dartmoor, describing the route from Meldon Reservoir car park and the memorial plaque installed in 2022 on the ridge above the crash site.
key_details: Walking route approximately 3 miles from Meldon Reservoir car park
Photographic documentation of wreckage and memorial

external_narratives.summary: Photo gallery documenting the tragic history of the US Navy PB4Y-1 Liberator on Dartmoor, with images of the crash site wreckage and the surrounding landscape in West Okement Valley.
key_details: Photographic documentation of wreckage fragments at crash site

external_narratives.summary: Historic England's Pastscape database entry for the crash site of PB4Y-1 Liberator 63926/E, recording it as a heritage feature on Dartmoor with formal archaeological documentation.
key_details: Formally recorded as heritage feature by Historic England
